risk_domain.name: Token Approval Phishing
risk_domain.description: Phishing attacks exploiting ERC-20/ERC-721 token approval mechanisms to drain user assets

limitations: cannot forge signatures without private key
malicious contracts are eventually flagged by security databases

H1_unlimited_approval_risk.name: Unlimited Approval Risk
H1_unlimited_approval_risk.description: Risk associated with approving unlimited token transfers, which could be used for unauthorized access.

signals.name: unlimited_approval
signals.description: Identifies calls to approve() or increaseAllowance() with extremely high values, potentially indicating unlimited approval.

signals.name: eip2612_unlimited
signals.description: Detects calls to EIP-2612 permit() or Permit2 with an unlimited amount.

signals.name: unverified_contract
signals.description: Detects the use of smart contracts that have not been verified on a block explorer and were deployed recently.

recommendations.description: Set maximum approved token transfers to reasonable limits.
Verify the smart contracts before deployment on a reputable block explorer.
Use security libraries that prevent unlimited approvals by default.
Regularly conduct code audits and peer reviews focusing on approval logic.

H2_unverified_contract_risk.name: Unverified Contract Risk
H2_unverified_contract_risk.description: Approving tokens to unverified contracts poses a high risk of falling victim to scams or malicious actors.

signals.name: unverified_spender
signals.description: Spender contract source code not verified on block explorer.

signals.name: new_spender_contract
signals.description: Spender contract deployed within last 24 hours.

recommendations.description: Reject any token transfers to unverified contracts.
Block transactions involving new spender contracts deployed recently.
Automatically verify the contract source code before approving token transfers.

H3_known_scam_address_risk.name: Known Scam Address Risk
H3_known_scam_address_risk.description: Interactions with known scam addresses are highly risky and may result in financial loss.

signals.name: known_scam_address_interactions
signals.description: Transactions sent to known scam addresses.

signals.name: frequent_interactions_with_scam_addresses
signals.description: Multiple transactions with known scam addresses within a short period of time.

signals.name: suspicious_transaction_amounts
signals.description: Transaction amounts that match common patterns used by scammers, such as very small or large values.

recommendations.description: Block all transactions to known scam addresses.
Regularly review and report any interactions with suspected scam addresses.
Utilize a blockchain analysis tool that filters out known scam addresses.
Automatically flag or block suspicious transaction amounts based on predefined thresholds.

H4_suspicious_function_selector.name: Suspicious Function Selector
H4_suspicious_function_selector.description: Use of non-standard function selectors can indicate hidden malicious activities or unexpected behavior.

signals.name: non_standard_selector
signals.description: Function selectors not present in official or commonly used contract interfaces.

signals.name: multicall_hidden_approval
signals.description: Use of multicall functions with hidden approval operations among others.

signals.name: unknown_operator_approval
signals.description: SetApprovalForAll function targeting an unknown or suspicious operator address.

recommendations.description: Exclude contracts using non-standard function selectors from your application.
Monitor and review the use of multicall functions in smart contracts.
Validate operator addresses for approval calls to ensure they are legitimate.
Implement Solidity security libraries to prevent the use of non-standard function selectors.
Run automated audits on smart contracts for suspicious function selectors and approvals.

H5_stale_approval_risk.name: Stale Approval Risk
H5_stale_approval_risk.description: Unrevoked approvals for contracts not used in a long time may expose users to potential risks.

signals.name: stale_approval_duration
signals.description: Tracks the duration since the last interaction with an approval.

signals.name: low_recent_activity
signals.description: Monitors low activity levels in a contract over a long period.

signals.name: unlimited_approval
signals.description: Detects approvals with an amount equal to the maximum value (2^256-1).

recommendations.description: Review approvals not used for a long time.
Revoke approvals that are no longer needed.
Set a time limit for approval validity.
Use a smart contract security tool to automatically detect and manage stale approvals.

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring phishing detection rate

methodology.step_1: Collect dataset of known phishing transactions (Forta alerts, Scam Sniffer reports)
methodology.step_2: Collect dataset of legitimate DeFi approval transactions
methodology.step_4: Measure: true positive rate (phishing caught), false positive rate (legitimate flagged)

combined_benchmark.target: Catch >90% of known phishing patterns with <5% false positive rate

templates.summary: Scanned {n_txs} approval transactions. {n_risky} flagged ({pct_risky}%). Value at risk: ${value_at_risk}. Top risks: {top_risks}.
